disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Large dairy load (milkshake plus cheese) consumed roughly 90 minutes before the symptoms began
trigger_question_examples: What did you eat or drink just before this started?
When exactly did the symptoms come on?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I had a big milkshake and some cheese, and it all kicked off maybe an hour and a half later.

trigger_question_examples: What are your bowel motions like?
Is there any blood in the diarrhoea?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's completely watery and explosive — there's no blood in it, just liquid.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe abdominal bloating and distension with excessive flatulence and loud borborygmi
trigger_question_examples: Is your tummy bloated or swollen?
Are you passing a lot of wind or hearing loud stomach noises?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: My belly is hugely bloated and swollen, there's tons of wind, and my stomach is making really loud noises.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Recurrent identical episodes 30 minutes to 2 hours after dairy over the past year, resolving completely with strict dairy avoidance
trigger_question_examples: Do certain foods reliably set it off, and does avoiding them help?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, it happens most times I have a lot of dairy, and it goes away completely when I stay off milk and cheese.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Pertinent negatives — no fever, no vomiting, no blood in stool, no weight loss, no chronic lethargy
trigger_question_examples: Have you had any fever, vomiting, blood in your stool, or unexplained weight loss?
Have you been unusually tired or losing weight over recent months?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No fever, I haven't vomited, there's no blood, and I haven't been losing weight or feeling run down otherwise.

trigger_question_examples: Do you feel sick or nauseous?
Have you been vomiting at all?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I feel a bit queasy but I haven't actually been sick.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Difficulty maintaining oral hydration because of the frequency of the diarrhoea
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to keep fluids down?
How often are you having to go to the toilet?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I keep running to the toilet so often it's hard to keep any fluids in.
